project_metadata.lockfile_format_note: bun.lockb is the binary lockfile format used by Bun <1.1 (header: '#!/usr/bin/env bun\nbun-lockfile-format-v0'). This probe now ships a REAL Bun 1.0.36-generated lockfile (~7.5KB), validated with `bun install --frozen-lockfile`. The earlier 100-byte placeholder is in git history only.
project_metadata.lockfile_note_peer_deps: Bun installs peerDependencies and optionalDependencies by default (inverse of npm/yarn/pnpm — see SCA-5896). elysia's peers (@sinclair/typebox, file-type, openapi-types) are therefore present in the lockfile and pull a transitive subtree (file-type -> @tokenizer/inflate/strtok3/token-types/uint8array-extras -> debug/ms/@tokenizer/token/@borewit/text-codec/ieee754). All 18 packages are real entries in bun.lockb.

mend_detection_prediction.notes: With a real binary lockfile in place, a correct Mend binary bun.lockb parser should emit the full 18-package tree (2 direct + 16 transitive) matching this expected-tree. This is the FM-4 'positive' path, now the documented CORRECT outcome rather than a surprise. If Mend lacks a binary bun.lockb parser, detection falls into FM-1/FM-2 (empty tree) — still the documented risk for this pair.
mend_detection_prediction.failure_mode_if_broken: EMPTY_TREE (no binary bun.lockb parser in Mend UA)

mend_expected_behavior.documented_limitation: Bun is not a UA-supported resolver. Whether Mend's JavaScript resolver carries a binary bun.lockb (Bun <1.1) parser is the exact question this probe answers. With a real lockfile now committed, the correct outcome is the full 18-package tree; absence of a binary parser shows up as an empty tree.

failure_modes.label: Empty tree + parse error in logs
failure_modes.description: Mend's npm-resolver fallback attempts to read bun.lockb as JSON/JSONC, fails immediately on the binary header bytes, logs a parse error, and emits an empty dependency tree.

failure_modes.label: Empty tree + silent skip
failure_modes.description: Mend detects bun.lockb is not a JSON file, silently skips it without logging an error, and emits an empty dependency tree. Worse than FM-1 because there is no diagnostic signal.

failure_modes.label: Phantom / corrupted packages from binary mis-parse
failure_modes.description: Mend's parser misinterprets binary bytes as partial tokens, producing phantom package names or corrupted version strings — bad data rather than empty output.
failure_modes.expected_tree: corrupted or phantom entries (does NOT match the 18-package tree)

failure_modes.label: Correct parse: binary bun.lockb fully resolved
failure_modes.description: Mend has a working binary bun.lockb parser. Output matches this expected-tree exactly: 18 packages (hono + elysia direct; cookie/memoirist/exact-mirror/fast-decode-uri-component as elysia regular deps; @sinclair/typebox/file-type/openapi-types as elysia peers; plus the file-type transitive subtree). With a real lockfile committed this is now the CORRECT/expected outcome, not a surprise.
failure_modes.expected_tree: non-empty — 18 packages, must match this expected-tree and sibling probe #10's package set

warnings: REAL LOCKFILE: bun.lockb is now a genuine Bun 1.0.36 binary lockfile (~7.5KB, bun-lockfile-format-v0), generated by running `bun@1.0.36 install` against this package.json and validated with `bun install --frozen-lockfile`. The previous 100-byte placeholder is retained only in git history.

EXPECTED POPULATED TREE: Because a real binary lockfile is present, the documented CORRECT outcome is the full 18-package tree (FM-4). An empty tree (FM-1/FM-2) now indicates Mend lacks a binary bun.lockb parser — that is the regression/finding this probe is designed to surface, not the baseline expectation.

PEER/OPTIONAL DEPS INCLUDED: Bun installs peerDependencies and optionalDependencies by default (inverse of npm/yarn/pnpm; see SCA-5896). The tree therefore includes elysia's peers (@sinclair/typebox, file-type, openapi-types) and their transitives. Do not flag these as phantom entries when comparing Mend output.

PAIR V1 — PROBE A: This is the Bun 1.0 (binary bun.lockb) side of version-variation pair V1. Same package.json as sibling probe #10 (bun-1.1-text-lock-probe). Only the lockfile format differs. NOTE: the sibling's expected-tree currently encodes a smaller 6-package set (elysia 1.4.28 without peer expansion); both probes share an identical package.json, so a like-for-like comparison should normalize on the resolved package SET. If this probe yields the full tree and the sibling yields fewer/none, investigate whether the difference is parser coverage (binary vs text) or stale sibling expected-tree.

Paired probe: bun-1.1-text-lock-probe (#10) at https://github.com/mend-detection-qa/bun-1.1-text-lock-probe. Same package.json (name=bun-version-pair-probe, hono ^4.0.0, elysia ^1.0.0); only the lockfile format differs.

Bun is NOT in the Mend install-tool supported list. scanSettings.versioning cannot pin the Bun toolchain. Detection MUST be lockfile-driven and entirely static — Mend reads bun.lockb, it does not re-run bun install.

VERSION DRIFT: resolved versions (hono@4.12.27, elysia@1.4.29, etc.) reflect the registry state at generation time (2026-06-25). Count assertions in autotest_config.json should use gte tolerances, not exact equality, to stay green across minor registry drift.
